item.title: Kelsey Grammer warns of chilling danger facing Americans who forget nation's founding history
item.description: The "Frasier" star pushes back against critics who dismiss the Founding Fathers as "old White guys in wigs," noting Thomas Jefferson was 33 when he changed history.
item.content:encoded:
Actor Kelsey Grammer issued a warning about the state of American education, arguing that citizens who fail to learn the nation's founding history risk being manipulated.

Speaking on "Planet Tyrus" Tuesday, Grammer called for an overhaul of the nation's education system while pushing back against critics who dismiss the Founding Fathers as "old White guys in wigs."

"If you don't know where you come from, you don't know who you are. If you don't know who you are, you can be controlled," Grammer warned.

SPEAKER JOHNSON WARNS STUDENTS ARE 'INDOCTRINATED' TO SEE US THROUGH 'WRONG LENSES' AS NATION CELEBRATES 250TH

"There's a whole bunch of things that just have got to stop with our educational system, and we got to retool it. There's no question," he said.

Grammer recently starred in the film, "Young Washington," which follows the Founding Father before he became the nation's first president. Grammer also hosted the six-episode Fox Nation series "The Patriot War," narrating key points in the American Revolution ahead of the nation's 250th anniversary.

The former "Frasier" and "Cheers" actor said America's past serves as a blueprint for its future — one he believes many young people are dismissing.

KELSEY GRAMMER URGES AMERICANS TO LOOK TO FOUNDING FATHERS FOR GUIDANCE AS NATION FACES DEEP DIVISIONS

"Yeah, I'm pretty tired of the old White guy in [a] wig thing," Grammer told Tyrus. "I mean Thomas Jefferson was 33 when he wrote what is arguably the most important political document in the history of man," he said.

Grammer emphasized that the American Revolution was fought on radical ideas rather than identity and called for a stronger push to teach early American history.

"It never had anything to do with the way you looked," Grammer said. "It had everything to do with the way you thought and the way you dreamed, and that's all America."

WATCH THE FULL 'PLANET TYRUS' INTERVIEW ON YOUTUBE

He argued the Founding Fathers did not claim America would be perfect but envisioned an evolving quest for better.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE COVERAGE OF MEDIA AND CULTURE

"The land of the possible. Yeah, absolutely. It's always [going to] fall a little short, but that's just it. It's the world of becoming. We're becoming a more perfect union, always, rather than we just are one," Grammer said.

Grammer said that most of the 56 people who signed the Declaration of Independence "ended in ruin" by sacrificing everything for the nation. He added, "Some of their children were murdered, some of their wives were raped," and said their spirit of resilience in hard times remains today.

"They paid the ultimate sacrifice, the ultimate one. And they would probably do it again. And I think that sort of character is what still is in most of us. I believe in America that way," Grammer said.

item.title: Jason Alexander apologizes after Courtney Stodden says 2012 comedy sketch objectified her as a minor
item.description: Jason Alexander apologized to Courtney Stodden after she shared a photo from a 2012 sketch where he rubbed a cellphone across her chest when she was 17.
item.content:encoded:
Jason Alexander is taking accountability for his past mistakes.

The 66-year-old actor recently apologized to Courtney Stodden for participating in a 2012 Funny or Die sketch in which he held a cellphone up to her chest and made comments about her body when she was a minor.

"Looking back at the comedy sketch in which Ms. Stodden and I participated in 2012, I completely agree that it was inappropriate and I truly regret it," he told Fox News Digital in a statement. "But more importantly, I am deeply sorry for any harm or distress it has caused Ms. Stodden. I offer her my sincerest apologies."

His apology comes one day after Stodden called out Alexander on Instagram as one of the writers of the sketch, adding, "As an adult, that fact has become harder—not easier—for me to understand."

GOLDIE HAWN SOUNDS THE ALARM ON CHILDHOOD CELEBRITY CULTURE: 'ALL I CAN SAY IS IT'S A NIGHTMARE'

In the Instagram post, she shared a photo of her and Alexander in the sketch, in which she is wearing a bikini, and the actor is rubbing a cellphone across her chest.

"I was 17 years old in this photo. The older I get, the more impossible this is to understand," she wrote. "During this comedy sketch, Jason Alexander repeatedly rubbed his phone across my breasts while saying there was 'no signal' in my head but 'plenty of signal' in my chest. Later in the sketch, he joked that when I turned 18 he’d like to take me behind the couch and 'have his way' with me."

She added that as a minor she "didn’t have the legal power to decide whether I wanted to participate" and that "the contracts were signed by adults."

At the time, she was married to Doug Hutchison, who she tied the knot with when she was 16 and he was 51, noting the paycheck for this appearance went to him.

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

"I was the only child in the room. I felt so alone," she added. "Looking back now, I don’t see comedy. I see a 17-year-old girl whose body became part of an adult joke. People still say, 'You chose to be there.' Legally, I couldn’t make those decisions for myself. That’s the point."

She ended the post by acknowledging that while she isn't able to change what she went through, she finds herself asking, "How did so many adults look at a minor and decide this was acceptable?"

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

"That’s exactly why I speak out today," she concluded. "Children deserve laws that protect them. They shouldn’t have to become adults before realizing the law should have protected them all along."

The caption also included a call to support California Assembly Bill 1267, which aims to permanently end child marriage by setting the minimum legal age for marriage in California to 18 with no exceptions.

Stodden and Hutchison got married in 2011 despite their 34-year age gap. They split up and got back together a few times before officially calling it quits in 2016, although divorce papers were not filed until March 2018, with the divorce being finalized in March 2020.

She announced their divorce in an Instagram post featuring her wedding photo, calling it "an emotional day," but assured her fans "it’s for the better."

"I look back at this picture and feel absolutely taken advantage of," she wrote. "I’ve been scared to even speak up about feeling groomed or being verbally abused during the almost 10 year marriage because I was a child and he was 50 when we married but I’m a woman now and it’s time for me to put my big girl pants on and speak on this matter."

She continued: "I’ve felt completely trapped, manipulated and at times abandoned by adults // growing up in such an environment — it became a lonely and dark place."

item.title: JONATHAN TURLEY: Judge Boasberg whines about Trump’s power and then gets caught exceeding his own
item.description: Pro-life groups appeal after Judge James Boasberg again denied them a jury trial over alleged selective enforcement against their chalk protest.
item.content:encoded:
Chief Judge James Boasberg is frustrated ... and he is not alone. Recently, the D.C. federal judge reportedly groused that the D.C. appellate court had ordered him to reconsider a case about whether the D.C. government engaged in biased enforcement against political graffiti. Conversely, the litigants are equally irked over what they see as Boasberg's bias after he responded by, again, dismissing their claims without a trial.

For a second time, Boasberg has barred pro-life groups from access to a jury after members were arrested for writing in chalk, "Black Pre-Born Lives Matter" on a sidewalk while Black Lives Matter writings were left untouched.

I wrote earlier about the emerging body of "Boasberg's Law," cases in which the judge has increasingly shown an untethered and expansive view of his own authority. Recently, Boasberg was found to have committed an "abuse of discretion" in twice finding that the Trump administration was in contempt of court for failing to turn around deportation flights.

DC APPEALS COURT ORDERS JUDGE BOASBERG TO HALT TRUMP CONTEMPT PROBE OVER DEPORTATION FLIGHTS

My disagreements with Boasberg are not over the underlying issues, but rather the court's rejection of basic limits on its own authority. While chastising President Donald Trump for exceeding his authority, Boasberg has been reversed for exceeding his own.

In the earlier case, Boasberg dismissed grand jury subpoenas in the probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell. I have been critical of that probe, but Boasberg's refusal to allow the subpoenas to be executed was based on an opinion that was rife with open hostility for Trump, dubious sources and biased observations.

I have similar reservations in this latest case. During the Black Lives Matter protests of 2020, streets in Washington were covered with BLM graffiti and the police watched as protesters wrote slogans and slurs on stores, streets and sidewalks. The government itself even commissioned a massive painting of the message on a street near the White House.

GOP LAWMAKERS URGE SUPREME COURT TO STRENGTHEN FIRST AMENDMENT PROTECTIONS FOR STREET PREACHERS

However, when the Frederick Douglass Foundation and Students for Life of America wrote their pro-life version of the slogan, they were immediately arrested and the chalk protest removed.

The D.C. Circuit's reversal of Boasberg in the case was a major victory for free speech. Boasberg had applied the wrong standard to kill the case, holding that the group could not challenge the selective enforcement of the laws. In reaching that conclusion, Boasberg applied the wrong standard, imposing the heavy burden of proving that the city was engaged in "invidious enforcement." While that standard was used correctly to dismiss an equal protection claim, it is not the standard for a free speech challenge.

The court did not hide its dissatisfaction with the thrust of Boasberg's opinion in dismissing the First Amendment claim:

"The First Amendment prohibits government discrimination on the basis of viewpoint. 'To permit one side … to have a monopoly in expressing its views … is the antithesis of constitutional guarantees.' The protection for freedom of speech applies not only to legislation, but also to enforcement of the laws."

The appellate court sent the case back to Boasberg with the opportunity to apply the correct standard and to create a substantive record before issuing a ruling on the free speech claim.

Boasberg took the case and ruled again that the groups would not be allowed a trial. The new dismissal was issued despite the standard that, in seeking such a dismissal before a trial, the court must accept all material facts in favor of the nonmoving party or, in this case, the pro-life and pro-free-speech litigants.

Boasberg rejected the factual claim that they were "similarly situated" to the BLM protesters. The litigants presented the results of discovery on the point, but Boasberg dismissed the claim on his own subjective view of the evidence.

They have now filed again with the appellate court to appeal his order.

Some of Boasberg's factual findings are baffling. For example, he maintains that the groups did not show that MPD officers "even witnessed any specific defacement during [Black Lives Matter] protests." However, the groups presented such evidence as bodycam video of officers watching as a protester spray-painted 'I can’t breathe' on the street near the White House two weeks after the arrests of the pro-life protesters. They submitted other video evidence of officers watching protesters spray-paint BLM messages outside of the Department of Justice.

Moreover, they presented what they claimed was guidance from Assistant Chief of Police Jeffery Carroll, in charge of the Metropolitan Police Department's Special Operations Division, in the application of different treatment for graffiti.

Carroll reportedly admitted to being "present" at BLM protests featuring "defacement" of property, public and private, and Lt. Jason Bagshaw admitted that he also saw defacement "related to" BLM protests.

This does not mean that a jury would rule for these litigants, but rather that the court cannot substitute its judgment for a jury when a credible and supported claim has been made by litigants.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE FOX NEWS OPINION

Boasberg declares that no such credible evidence was presented. The fact is that a reasonable jury could conclude that there was different treatment shown in the enforcement of these laws based on the content of the speech.

The biased enforcement of laws to crack down on speech is a common reality in other countries where courts enable such selective enforcement. In countries such as Iran and China, no level of evidence is sufficient to overcome the bias of courts in showing a preference for one side.

Judge Boasberg has had a distinguished career that is now being undone by a lack of restraint in these decisions. Again, I believe that the judge has been on solid ground in some of these conflicts, including criticizing the use of "intemperate and disrespectful" language in filings and demanding compliance with his orders. However, while Chief Judge Boasberg has described the president's claim of expansive powers as "awfully frightening," the same can be said about a judge who substitutes his own judgment for juries and disregards legal standards for disfavored groups. The Unfinished Story of the American Revolution."

CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM JONATHAN TURLEY

item.title: Former Bernie Sanders official accuses senator of pushing most antisemitic candidates 'in a generation'
item.description: Joel Rubin, who led Jewish outreach for Sen. Bernie Sanders' 2020 campaign, told Jewish Insider the senator is pushing antisemitic candidates into the Democratic Party.
item.content:encoded:
Former campaign official for Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., Joel Rubin called out the Vermont senator last week for pushing the "most categorically anti-Israel, antisemitic candidates" he's seen in recent years.

"I think what Sanders and the Sanders operation have done is shove into our system the most categorically anti-Israel, antisemitic candidates that we’ve seen in a generation — if ever, certainly within the Democratic Party — and it’s by design," Rubin said in an interview with Jewish Insider. 

"The goal is to undermine Democratic unity and even rationality on Israel at this stage," Rubin said.

SANDERS UNDER FIRE FOR PROPPING UP PLATNER AS DEMS TORCH HIS TOXIC ENDORSEMENT 'PATTERN'

Rubin, who was also a State Department official under President Barack Obama, previously led Jewish outreach for Sanders' 2020 presidential campaign. However, following Sanders' past support for former Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner, who sported a tattoo resembling a Nazi "totenkopf," a skull-and-crossbones design, Rubin has since concluded that his former boss is now intentionally pushing antisemitism.

Rubin went as far as to refer to Sanders as an "outright hostile actor" pushing a "hate-based narrative" surrounding Israel along with several rising Democratic politicians.

"I’m extremely disappointed. I feel like it violates the goals of promoting peace in the Middle East and the two-state solution, and I believe that it exacerbates the pain and distress that the American Jewish community is experiencing right now in Israel policy," Rubin said. "I had higher hopes for a senator who is Jewish, and his team who knows very well what’s out there."

FETTERMAN DEMANDS BERNIE SANDERS APOLOGIZE FOR BACKING ALLEGED 'PREDATOR' GRAHAM PLATNER IN MAINE SENATE RACE

He warned that these efforts would leave Democrats vulnerable in elections against the Republican Party, particularly as support for Israel appears to be a new breaking point for progressive candidates.

"I don’t know what’s going on inside that operation. There is no line. There is no bottom. There is no low," Rubin said. "And what it’s doing now is gutting American Jewish confidence in the Democratic Party, which is not a way to win elections, and it’s unethical as well, considering American Jews have been a bedrock community supporting the Democratic Party."

"We need leadership, and the progressive community needs to stop scapegoating Jews for all problems in the world," he added.

CLICK HERE TO FOLLOW FOX NEWS MEDIA AND CULTURE ON FACEBOOK

Fox News Digital reached out to Sanders' office for comment but did not immediately hear back.

Along with Platner, Sanders has campaigned for and endorsed Melat Kiros in Colorado and Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan, who have both criticized Israel for committing an alleged "genocide" during its war in Gaza.

Sanders was also one of several Democratic members of Congress who have supported efforts to cut $3 billion in aid to Israel.

item.title: Dem Rep Seth Moulton says party has no immigration plan, is only ‘opposition to Trump’
item.description: Rep. Seth Moulton says Democrats lack a clear immigration plan and rely too heavily on opposing President Donald Trump, revealing town hall responses from Massachusetts voters.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic Rep. Seth Moulton, D-Mass., acknowledged Tuesday on MS NOW's "Money, Power, Politics" that Democrats have failed to give voters a clear immigration plan and rely too heavily on opposition to President Donald Trump, as he discussed the party's challenges in Arizona's gubernatorial primary.

"We don’t have a plan for immigration. We’re not able to say what, actually, Democrats should do to fix the problem," Moulton said. "All we are is opposition to Trump."

The Massachusetts congressman and U.S. Senate candidate said he had tested voters' understanding of the party's position during town halls across his state.

BIDEN ADMINISTRATION FORCED A FALSE CHOICE ON AMERICANS ABOUT IMMIGRATION

"I’ll ask people, ‘How many of you are opposed to Trump’s immigration enforcement?’ Every hand in the room goes up," Moulton said, adding, "Like, I’ve asked thousands of people in Massachusetts this question."

The congressman also faulted Democrats for dismissing other voter concerns, including inflation, during former President Joe Biden's administration.

"Under Biden, we said inflation is not really a big deal. It’s transitory. It’s going to go away," Moulton said. "The point is we have this history of kind of just like washing over problems and not addressing real concerns that voters have."

DYING FORMER CONGRESSMAN BARNEY FRANK TELLS DEMOCRATS THEIR FAR-LEFT MESSAGING IS COSTING THEM VOTERS

Fox News Digital reached out to the Democratic National Committee for comment but did not immediately receive a response.

When host Stephanie Ruhle noted that Republicans benefited from having a defined immigration approach, Moulton contrasted Trump's enforcement agenda with his party's response.

"This is criminal what Trump is doing," Moulton said. "And the fact that we don’t even have an answer to that is a real problem."

HOUSE REPUBLICANS MOVE TO MAKE TRUMP'S BORDER POLICIES PERMANENT

Moulton outlined his own approach, calling for the abolition of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), prosecution of officials who break the law and reforms encouraging legal immigration.

"So, yes, abolish ICE, prosecute these people for breaking the law, but then show how we’re going to fix the problem," he said.

The congressman said the current system made the legal route unnecessarily difficult.

"Right now, it’s easier for people to come across the border than to go through the bureaucratic process," Moulton said.

Moulton's congressional website states that he supports comprehensive immigration reform that secures the border, curbs illegal immigration, expands legal pathways and provides a path to citizenship. In January, he backed legislation intended to allow ICE officers to be prosecuted for misconduct.

Rep. Moulton is challenging Sen. Ed Markey, D-Mass., in the state’s Sept. 1 Democratic Senate primary. A University of New Hampshire Survey Center poll conducted June 18-23 found Markey leading Moulton 41% to 35% among likely Democratic primary voters, with 23% undecided. The poll had a 5.1-point margin of error.

item.title: Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion
item.description: A manhunt is underway in Pima County after a woman was sexually assaulted during a home invasion about 30 miles from where Nancy Guthrie vanished.
item.content:encoded:
An Arizona manhunt is underway after a woman was sexually assaulted during a home invasion about 30 miles from where Nancy Guthrie vanished nearly six months ago.

Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos, whose office is investigating both cases, told Fox News Digital there is "nothing to indicate the cases are related."

The attack happened shortly after 2:40 a.m. Monday at a home in the 12000 block of West Vegas Drive, near Taylor Lane and Ajo Way, as first reported by local NBC affiliate KVOA, citing the Pima County Sheriff's Department.

PIMA COUNTY SHERIFF ISSUES ALERT FOR KIDNAPPING SUSPECT LESS THAN 10 MILES FROM NANCY GUTHRIE'S HOME

Deputies with the sheriff's San Xavier District responded to the home after receiving a report of an assault.

Authorities said the intruder entered through an unlocked door, physically and sexually assaulted the victim, then tried to burglarize the home before fleeing. No arrests had been announced as of Tuesday.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

The suspect is described as either Hispanic or White, about 5 feet 10 inches tall with a thin build. He has gray in his beard and was last seen wearing an oversized green shirt.

The home invasion occurred about 30 miles from the Catalina Foothills home where 84-year-old Guthrie, the mother of "Today" co-anchor Savannah Guthrie, disappeared on Feb. 1 in a case that remains under investigation by the FBI and the sheriff's department.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

Fox News Digital reported Tuesday that FBI Director Kash Patel said a four-day delay before the FBI joined the Guthrie investigation may have cost investigators crucial video evidence.

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

Patel accused the sheriff's department of keeping the bureau "out of the investigation" for four days after Guthrie's disappearance.

NANCY GUTHRIE SHERIFF SCOLDED COUNTY OFFICIAL FOR SHARING TIP WITH FBI, SHE CLAIMS IN INTERVIEW

Nanos has disputed Patel's account, saying the FBI was notified promptly and worked alongside investigators.

SEND US A TIP

Authorities in the Guthrie case have said the unidentified person seen outside her home was believed to be between 5 feet 9 inches and 5 feet 10 inches tall.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

The sheriff's department is urging residents who live near the crime scene to review home security or neighborhood surveillance footage that may help identify the suspect.

GET BREAKING NEWS BY EMAIL

Anyone with information about the home invasion is asked to call 911 or submit an anonymous tip to 88-CRIME.

The FBI did not immediately respond to Fox News Digital's request for comment on the investigation.

item.title: Republicans aren’t stopping: GOP to unleash another ‘big, beautiful bill’ with a new target
item.description: Steve Scalise confirms a fourth reconciliation bill targeting government waste and fraud, aiming to return money to taxpayers after passing 3.0.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX: The No. 2 leader in the House of Representatives has confirmed plans for a fourth "big, beautiful bill" that would tackle government waste and abuse of federal dollars.

"If you're a single waitress, working three shifts at a diner, Democrats voted against you keeping your tip money … so that they can give that money to fraudsters in Minnesota who were sending it to terrorist organizations," House Majority Leader Steve Scalise, R-La., told Fox News Digital, referring to Republicans' first sweeping party-line budget bill that President Donald Trump dubbed his "one big, beautiful bill."

"It's insane, but that was really going on. There’s more of it. We want to root that out. So we're gonna move yet another reconciliation bill to focus on things like rooting out waste and fraud, to give that money back to taxpayers."

TOP REPUBLICAN PUSHES FOR RECONCILIATION 3.0 TO ADDRESS AFFORDABILITY

The budget reconciliation process allows the party in power in Congress to pass sweeping fiscal policy legislation while completely sidelining the other side — in this case, Democrats.

It does so by bypassing the Senate filibuster — which needs 60 votes to overcome — allowing the upper chamber to line up with the House's own simple majority threshold.

But the measures in a reconciliation bill must adhere to a strict set of rules, such as not touching Social Security and only including policies that would affect the federal budget.

GOP'S $95B DEFENSE PACKAGE SURVIVES CRITICAL HURDLE AMID FISCAL HAWK REVOLT

Congressional Republicans have passed two reconciliation bills so far, one focused on Trump's tax priorities and another replenishing funds for the president's immigration crackdown.

The GOP is working on a third that would add some $95 billion in federal spending over the next 10 years. It includes $73 billion toward the U.S. war with Iran, $12 billion in aid for American farmers, and $10 billion for states to implement election security measures that were part of the SAVE America Act.

But some House fiscal hawks are threatening to oppose the measure over its lack of spending cuts to offset those dollars.

Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., and Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Director Russell Vought both pitched a fourth bill during a closed-door meeting on Tuesday morning in a bid to assuage those concerns, multiple people told Fox News Digital.

Scalise's comments are the first public confirmation from a member of House GOP senior leadership that such a bill was in the works.

"We want to move that as soon as we finish reconciliation 3.0. So when we were working on reconciliation 2.0, we were already starting to put together 3.0, but we waited until we passed 2.0. Then we went right to work on this latest reconciliation bill," Scalise said.

item.title: After Democrats' shake-up, Collins faces toss-up against Platner replacement in must-win Senate race
item.description: Susan Collins faces a fierce challenge from progressive Troy Jackson, who backs "Medicare-for-all" and abolishing ICE, in a toss-up Maine Senate race.
item.content:encoded:
The first Fox News Power Rankings of the 2026 midterm elections and a new poll both indicate an extremely close contest between longtime Republican Sen. Susan Collins of Maine and Troy Jackson, the candidate set to replace ex-nominee Graham Platner as the Democratic challenger, in what's expected to be a premiere Senate battleground.

The Fox News Power Rankings, which gauge the competitiveness of elections using data, reporting and analysis, ranks the showdown in Maine as a toss-up. The midterm ballot box battle is one of roughly a dozen Senate contests that will determine if the Democrats win back the chamber or if Republicans hold onto their slim majority.

Meanwhile, a Pine Tree Poll conducted over the past seven days by the University of New Hampshire Survey Center indicates Jackson with a slight three-point edge over Collins among likely general election voters in Maine.

WHO'S UP AND WHO'S DOWN: CHECK OUT THE FOX NEWS POWER RANKINGS ON THE MIDTERM ELECTIONS

Jackson, a former Maine Senate president, is on a glide path to replace Platner at Saturday's Maine Democratic Party nominating convention in Bangor, after winning the vast majority of the 500 voting delegates chosen this past weekend by Democrats in the state's 16 counties. And most of Jackson's top rivals for the nomination backed him after dropping out of the race on Sunday.

While the delegates on Jackson's slate are not required to support him at the convention in Bangor, Maine, he is now considered all but certain to secure the nomination.

A fifth-generation logger and a one-time conservative Republican who became a Democrat over two decades ago, Jackson came in third place last month in Maine's Democratic gubernatorial primary.

Platner dropped out earlier this month, amid calls from top Democrats in Maine and across the country to immediately exit the race following an explosive report containing an allegation of rape from a woman he previously dated. Platner denied the report but stepped aside.

FIVE THINGS TO KNOW ABOUT TROY JACKSON, WHO'S ABOUT TO REPLACE GRAHAM PLATNER AS THE DEMOCRATS' SENATE NOMINEE

Jackson, who had campaigned alongside Platner during the primary, quickly made the case that he was the best equipped candidate to grab the mantle of the progressive and populist movement that had backed Platner.

Along with Platner, Jackson had the support of progressive champion Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont as he ran for governor, and he is currently backed by Our Revolution, the group born in 2016 out of Sanders' first presidential campaign.

Jackson, similar to Platner, is running on a platform aimed at working-class voters. He backs raising taxes on billionaires, the universal healthcare system known as "Medicare-for-all," abolishing U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, and he opposes military aid to Israel at a time that Democrats are increasingly divided on support for America's longtime Middle East ally.

Collins, as she runs for a sixth six-year term in the Senate, is facing a rough political climate for Republicans, amid GOP President Donald Trump's underwater approval ratings, persistent inflation and what polls indicate is an unpopular war with Iran.

DEMOCRACY ’26: STAY UP TO DATE WITH THE FOX NEWS ELECTION HUB

The senator, known as a moderate Republican who at times has voted against Trump, last month received bipartisan praise after reaching a milestone by casting her 10,000th consecutive Senate vote.

But Democrats are taking aim at Collins over her comments that she didn't regret her 2018 vote to confirm Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh, despite his crucial vote four years later to overturn the landmark Roe v. Wade abortion ruling.

And following the fatal shooting in Maine earlier this month involving Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents, Democrats heavily criticized the Senate Appropriations Committee chair for her support for ICE funding.

But Collins has a history of defeating the Democrats' efforts to oust her from the Senate.

Six years ago, she trailed Democratic challenger Sara Gideon, the then-Maine House speaker, but the senator ended up winning re-election by nearly 9 points.

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

Republicans currently hold a 53-47 majority in the U.S. Senate. That means Democrats would need a net gain of four seats in the midterms to retake the majority, which they lost in the 2024 elections.

The Senate race in Maine, along with battles for Republican-controlled Senate seats in Alaska, Iowa and Ohio, are ranked as toss-ups in the Fox News Power Rankings.

item.title: Democrats lure ‘politically homeless’ Gen Z toward socialism — a counterattack is taking shape
item.description: CJ Pearson says Gen Z voters are politically homeless and flirting with socialism, launching Next250 to counter the left on college campuses.
item.content:encoded:
Democrats are targeting "politically homeless" young people by offering them a sense of belonging, conservative activist CJ Pearson warned, arguing that the left is aggressively steering Gen Z toward socialism and DEI.

"When I look at why young people are at least flirting with socialism right now, I think it's because they're politically homeless. I think they're looking for a place where they feel heard, where they feel seen," said Pearson.

Pearson’s warning comes as he launches Next250, a political action committee aimed at organizing young conservatives, registering voters in swing states and countering support for socialism and DEI among Gen Z. The PAC also plans to spend $1 million on digital ads targeting young and minority voters in Georgia.

Pearson said Democrats have been more aggressive than conservatives in reaching young voters who feel politically alienated or uncertain about where they belong.

SOCIALISM’S RISE INSIDE THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY NOW THREATENS THE AMERICAN DREAM

"The left is vigilant," he said. "Every single day, they are trying to indoctrinate America’s young people. Every single day, they’re trying to pollute their minds with untruths and just downright delusion."

Pearson pointed to New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who has embraced the democratic socialist label and supported government-run grocery stores, rent freezes and universal child care.

The movement also gained ground when three congressional candidates endorsed by Mamdani — Brad Lander, Claire Valdez and Darializa Avila Chevalier — won their Democratic primaries.

"What I am saying to my generation who are looking at folks like Mamdani as potential solutions to a problem," said Pearson.

MAMDANI DOUBLES DOWN ON DEMOCRATIC SOCIALISM WHILE INSISTING WALL STREET, BUSINESS LEADERS CAN WORK WITH HIM

Pearson said he sees a similar dynamic at play in the debate over diversity, equity and inclusion, arguing the policies have fostered what he called the "soft bigotry of low expectations."

"The left loves to put young Black men like me in boxes. I'm a young Black man who grew up in the Deep South to a mother who chose life, who was raised by his grandparents," said Pearson.

Despite what he described as the left’s assumptions about his background, Pearson said the values he learned growing up — including faith, patriotism and a strong work ethic — made conservatism a natural fit.

KEY TRUMP AGENCY PUTS TOP UNIVERSITIES IN CROSSHAIRS OVER ALLEGED RACE-BASED DORM PROGRAMS

Since taking office, the Trump administration has sought to roll back DEI initiatives throughout the federal government, arguing the programs promote discrimination and merit should guide hiring and admissions decisions.

Pearson said he is building on his success organizing events for young conservatives to launch Next250, a political action committee aimed at countering support for socialism and DEI on college campuses.

His PAC will go head-to-head with the ideology embraced by younger students by rallying conservatives in deep blue areas and swing states for voter registration.

He sees partnering with fraternities and sororities as a way to build a force capable of pushing back against socialism and DEI.

"We're going to take this MAGA party on the road. We're going go to swing states like Georgia, Ohio, Michigan, Iowa. And we're going to throw these events and we're going to welcome young people around to them and we are going to have voter registration. We are going get them to the polls," said Pearson.

He added that the left's embrace of socialism and DEI candidates has already backfired for them.

The left wants to "accuse us being a Nazi. Well, that man had a Nazi tattoo on his chest and Democrats had no problem with it," said Pearson referencing former Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner who withdrew his candidacy after a bombshell rape allegation.

Pearson also plans to deploy $1 million for digital ads that target both young and minority voters in his home state of Georgia.

"America has been on a generational run, a 250-year run that was marketed by innovation, opportunity, and truly the hallmark and the pillars of the American dream. But if the left gets their way, there will not be another 250 years," said Pearson.

item.title: DOJ gives New Jersey 5 days to hand over voter data after noncitizen registration scandal
item.description: Harmeet Dhillon gave Gov. Mikie Sherrill five days to hand over voter data on 6,600 noncitizen registrations as the DOJ escalates its lawsuit.
item.content:encoded:
Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon doubled down on the DOJ's demand for New Jersey's voter registration data Wednesday after Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill's public revelation that around 6,600 noncitizens were added to the rolls through the motor-voter process.

Dhillon’s letter to Sherrill came as a slew of lawmakers called for audits and bipartisan cooperation to address the findings, while the Justice Department noted it remains in litigation against Lt. Gov. Dale Caldwell seeking New Jersey’s electronic voter database.

"Ensuring that U.S. citizens' votes are not illegally diluted by noncitizens' votes is of paramount importance," Dhillon wrote.

"We therefore write to formally request additional information to assess New Jersey's compliance with various federal statutes, as provided in our July 7, 2026, letter to the Lieutenant Governor. As we stated in that letter, we stand ready to help New Jersey election officials comply with the Help America Vote Act of 2002 and the National Voter Registration Act of 1993, as outlined in our memorandum titled, Federal Law Requirements for State and Local Election Officials."

DEMOCRAT NEW JERSEY GOVERNOR SAYS THOUSANDS OF NONCITIZENS DISCOVERED ON VOTER ROLLS, PURGED

Dhillon noted that about 400 of the roughly 6,600 noncitizens New Jersey said were mistakenly registered to vote and cast ballots.

She gave Sherrill five days to provide the names, personal information, registration dates and precincts of the 6,600, as well as the methods and times by which the 400 who voted cast their ballots, while treating the letter as a legal preservation-of-records request typical in litigation.

With the DOJ already engaged in litigation against Caldwell and the state, Dhillon said she would notify the judge in that case of the new development as the department continues to seek statewide voter registration lists from states including New Jersey, New York and Pennsylvania.

NEW JERSEY REMOVES 6,600 PEOPLE FROM VOTER ROLLS WHO WERE ELIGIBLE TO CAST A BALLOT FOR 2024 ELECTION

When reached for comment, the DOJ provided a separate letter Deputy Attorney General Jesus Osete sent to Caldwell and New Jersey Division of Elections Director Donna Barber on Wednesday, again requesting electronic voter registration data.

Osete said the DOJ has grounding to "ascertain New Jersey's compliance with the list maintenance requirements" of the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 and the Help America Vote Act of 2002.

He added that the New Jersey Division of Elections must also verify that no additional noncitizens are registered on the state's voter rolls.

The letter was filed as an exhibit in the Justice Department's ongoing lawsuit against New Jersey seeking access to the state's electronic voter registration database.

Meanwhile, the French technology contractor that works with the State Motor Vehicle Commission (MVC) said late Wednesday that responsibility lies not with the MVC but with the Department of State, led by Caldwell, and the Division of Elections.

Sherrill spokesman Sean Higgins slammed the DOJ in comments to Fox News Digital on Thursday, saying the Trump administration has a "long history of weaponizing" the agency for political purposes and of attempting to "overturn election results."

"Governor Sherrill has been clear that she will use all the legal tools available to her to protect New Jerseyans' personal information," Higgins said, pointing again to Sherrill's public remarks Wednesday and noting the governor's "disgust" at the vendor and former Gov. Philip Murphy's administration.

IDEMIA said in a statement it has worked with the MVC for four decades to process eligible individuals seeking to register to vote when conducting drivers licensing business.

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

"IDEMIA’s role is to transmit information through the motor vehicle system. The voter registration information is transmitted to the New Jersey Department of State, Division of Elections, which is ultimately responsible for verifying eligibility to vote," said the company — whose American base is in Reston, Virginia.

"Information submitted by IDEMIA must still be validated and adjudicated by the Division of Elections."

Meanwhile, the top Republican on the state's Judiciary Committee said she has tried multiple times to press the issue of voter-roll integrity with Democrats — who control all branches of government — since 2019.

"I have been warning... New Jersey needed stronger safeguards to protect the integrity of our elections," state Sen. Kristin Corrado, R-Totowa, said.

"I sent six separate letters raising concerns and calling for action and introduced legislation that would have provided a commonsense safeguard by preventing the MVC from prompting non-citizens to register to vote but my warnings were repeatedly ignored by the Democrats," she said in a statement that included some of the letters sent to the Murphy administration.

"Now we are learning that a catastrophic government failure allowed thousands of non-citizens to be registered to vote, and suddenly everyone is acting surprised," she said.

The DOJ appeared to signal that the New Jersey case strengthens the Trump administration's push for stronger election integrity measures nationwide as multiple states continue to resist requests for information sharing.

item.title: Pat McAfee forced to address ESPN layoffs on air during awkward segment, but the answer is simple
item.description: JJ Watt joked about ESPN layoffs on Pat McAfee's show, asking if everybody was fired to pay his reported $30 million salary and potential raise.
item.content:encoded:
Things look a little different at ESPN today. Haven't you heard?

The World Wide Leader laid off dozens of employees this week, including Ryan Clark, Cam Newton, Tom Pelissero and Karl Ravech. Others were let go as well, but those are some of the big names circulating the internet as of today.

This isn't the first round of layoffs at ESPN — or for any company. Frankly, this is at least the third or fourth round of layoffs at ESPN since I started working at OutKick four years ago. It's become a yearly story at this point.

EXCLUSIVE: ESPN TO PART WAYS WITH CAM NEWTON AS PART OF TALENT LAYOFFS THAT ALSO INCLUDED RYAN CLARK

Anyway, something else that's become a yearly topic is why ESPN is letting folks go, with several people placing the blame on Pat McAfee. That's right. Pat McAfee!

He earns roughly $30 million a year, and is reportedly in negotiations with ESPN to double that moving forward. Obviously, those numbers are going to raise some eyebrows when these layoffs happen.

NFL Hall of Famer JJ Watt joined the "progrum" on Tuesday, and ... didn't shy away from the elephant in the room:

"Slow, slow news day over there on the ESPN airwaves, huh?" Watt said to begin the interview. "I mean, besides the fact that you’re getting $100 million… Did they keep anybody? Or is everybody fired to pay you? I’m trying to figure that out."

Goodness gracious. Just a vicious way to start an interview. Brutal stuff.

Look, it was obviously in jest, and McAfee responded by laughing it off. He's not stupid. He's not immune to the narrative on social media right now.

"We do not appreciate that," he said. "That is the narrative, and I’m certainly taking it on the shins. The boys and I are very proud of the work that we have done. Thanks."

Here's the bottom line: ESPN views Pat McAfee as an asset. The network has put him everywhere since the two partnered up a few years back.

He's on College GameDay. He's on the sidelines for the biggest college football games, sometimes even getting his own simulcast feed. His show is on the main channel every day. He travels to big sporting events outside of football for his show.

Just last week, he was at the Home Run Derby, which was notable given it was the first year it wasn't on ESPN in decades.

It's clear that ESPN believes in McAfee and wants to continue to invest in him. It's probably not a bad investment to make given he's arguably the biggest all-around sports personality in the media.

He's also self-made. ESPN did not create Pat McAfee. ESPN invested in McAfee — and his team — years ago. Since he joined College GameDay in 2022, ratings have gone up. It's not all because of McAfee, of course, but that's not a coincidence.

"The first time we licensed the show through ESPN, one of these happened, we got blamed," McAfee said later in the interview. "This time, we’re getting blamed as well. Which I guess I can understand, but I mean, there’s billions and billions of dollars in these companies, and hard decisions have to get made.

"And we don’t like any of them, and we hope we’re an asset to the company going forward."

And that's what McAfee is to ESPN — an asset. A big one. He's proven that, time and time again, since joining the network. Think about all the big interviews you see out of the World Wide Leader right now ... where do they come from?

The Pat McAfee show. Not First Take. Not NFL Live. Not SportsCenter.

McAfee's radio show.

Think of all the viral moments we see every Saturday during the fall. Where do they come from?

McAfee's kicking segment during College GameDay. Or McAfee's outlandish intro during the opening scene.

Like it or hate it — and I know plenty of folks hate it — it's clearly working.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

Pat McAfee didn't cost Ryan Clark his job. Ryan Clark lost his job because he brought nothing to the table for ESPN, beyond an occasional headache because he said something stupid.

ESPN chose to cut ties with a liability, and put more money into an asset. That's not a new or novel concept.

It's how businesses have been run for years.

item.title: Beloved artist stabbed, killed in 'random' beach town daytime attack as police arrest 19-year-old suspect
item.description: Tyler Leibelt, 19, faces murder and violent felony allegation charges after the random stabbing death of a 73-year-old woman at Capitola Mall.
item.content:encoded:
Police are investigating a "random" attack where a woman was stabbed to death in the parking lot of a shopping mall in a California beach town.

Rachell Summers, 73, from Capitola, was discovered with multiple stab wounds outside the food court of the Capitola Mall; it happened just before 1 p.m. Monday.

The mall is located a little more than a mile from Capitola Beach, a popular beach resort in the Santa Cruz area.

AMERICAN TOURISTS STABBED OUTSIDE ATHENS' ACROPOLIS AS POLICE DETAIN SUSPECT IN APPARENT RANDOM ATTACK

The Capitola Police Department announced suspect Tyler Leibelt, 19, was arrested on charges related to the knife attack.

FOLLOW US ON X

Police said, "Based on the investigation, detectives continue to believe this was a random act of violence, and there is no indication that the suspect knew the victim prior to the attack."

CLICK HERE FOR MORE U.S. NEWS

According to court records, Leibelt is charged with murder, among other felonies.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

One of the charges listed, "PC667.5(C)-ENH-VIOLENT FELONY ALLEGATION" or California Penal Code 667.5, which reveals the suspect may have been charged in a previous felony case.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

That means Leibelt, if convicted in this case, may be subject to substantially longer incarceration times and stricter rules.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

GET BREAKING NEWS BY EMAIL

As for the victim, Summers was a local artist who managed the Art of Santa Cruz art gallery, which is now closed for the remainder of the week, the New York Post reports.

"Our hearts remain with Rachell's family, friends, and all those affected by this tragic and senseless act of violence," the police department said in a social post.

GET BREAKING NEWS ALERTS

The investigation is ongoing.

SEND US A TIP

item.title: Inside NYC's 'perfect scam': Luxury cars, dusty shelves and the EBT secret behind the counter
item.description: Fraud expert says New York City bodegas with expired food and skimming devices are secretly operating as "EBT ATMs" for organized criminals.
item.content:encoded:
Stocked with food that expired years ago and lined with dusty shelves, some New York City bodegas and delis have allegedly been operating as fronts for organized Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) card-skimming schemes, using hidden devices to steal information and ultimately taxpayer-funded benefits, according to an expert who is sounding the alarm.

Criminals attach hidden skimming devices to checkout payment terminals to capture EBT card data while using pinhole cameras or keypad overlays to record customers' PINs, according to the FBI. The stolen information is then used to create counterfeit cards and drain Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits, costing consumers and financial institutions more than $1 billion each year.

"A large percentage of these convenience stores are actually EBT ATMs," Haywood Talcove, CEO of LexisNexis Risk Solutions Government, told Fox News Digital "Their sole purpose is to traffic cards, not sell food."

Speaking after joining the Secret Service on a recent fraud operation in New York City, Talcove continued: "I saw firsthand a can of tuna that expired three years ago. I saw firsthand pancake syrup that was two years expired, sardines that were expired. What struck me is the industry that is built around trafficking these EBT cards, taking away money from individuals and their families that actually need it."

SNAP FOOD STAMP FRAUD HAS NEFARIOUS TERRORISM LINKS, TOP AGRICULTURE WATCHDOG WARNS CONGRESS

According to the Secret Service, the agency's New York Field Office conducted a three-day operation across Brooklyn, the Bronx and Queens in June that uncovered 35 illegal skimming devices and prevented an estimated $36.5 million in fraud, marking its fourth anti-skimming operation in New York City this year.

Eligible households receive monthly SNAP benefits on an EBT card, which works like a debit card and is used to buy eligible food at authorized retailers, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.

Talcove said he saw multiple stores on the same block with shelves stocked with expired food, which led him to believe some were operating primarily to facilitate EBT fraud rather than as legitimate grocery sales. During the operation, the Secret Service uncovered skimming devices on checkout payment terminals that can capture EBT card data that criminals can encode onto counterfeit cards for unauthorized purchases or withdrawals.

"The logical question you ask yourself is, 'How can there be enough economic activity to sustain stores that are all selling the same thing and they're very small?'" he said.

Talcove said "that's not why they're there," adding the condition of many stores led him to believe some businesses were operating primarily to facilitate EBT fraud rather than as legitimate grocery stores.

"They're there to not provide nutrition because across the street is a wonderful supermarket with fresh food and fresh fish and produce and shelves that aren't dusty that don't sell food that's expired," he said.

Although the fraud may appear to be carried out by only a small group, Talcove said it runs much deeper than that.

"You start looking at these groups and you very quickly realize they're criminal and they're organized," he said.

FRAUD EXPERT WARNS AI IS HELPING CRIMINALS OUTPACE THE GOVERNMENT: 'DON'T HAVE THE RIGHT TOOLS'

Michael Peck, assistant special agent in charge of the U.S. Secret Service's Criminal Investigative Division, told The City Reporter in June that criminals have packed as much as $1.2 million in stolen cash into dishwashers before shipping it overseas, and that the runners installing skimming devices earn about $150.

The City Reporter, citing the Secret Service, also reported that many of the organized crime groups behind the EBT skimming schemes originate in Eastern Europe and are "living large off the scam, renting $10,000-a-month apartments and Lamborghinis."

However, Talcove said the fraud extends beyond bodegas and delis, arguing many other retailers fit "into this category." He also said the SNAP program does not require itemized purchase records, making suspicious transactions harder to detect.

"I've got firsthand insights into one that was pretending to be a store, but it was actually a laundromat," he said. "I'm looking at one right now on the West Coast that is pretending to be a store that is actually a strip club accepting EBT."

Talcove called the fraud the "perfect scam," citing weak identity verification, outdated card technology, and limited purchase records.

"Number one, you're not going to get caught, number two, the government never runs out of money, and number three, the SNAP program has fundamental flaws in its administration," he said.

But Talcove said the "real victim here" is the families on SNAP who don't receive the food they need, something he said he witnessed firsthand in New York City. According to the U.S. Department of Agriculture, more than 250,000 retailers nationwide are authorized to accept SNAP benefits.

"The problem there is they have families," he said. "They have children that are hungry and the money that was intended to go to the child is being diverted to something that it was never intended to do."

Emphasizing the impact, Talcove said hard-earned taxpayer dollars are being lost while criminals take advantage of those "who show up every day, that try to do the right thing."

ALLEGED FEEDING OUR FUTURE FRAUD RINGLEADER TRANSFERRED FROM SOMALIA TO FACE US CHARGES

"They're transnational in nature," he said. "They are taking advantage of our empathy because they understand that you're not gonna stop somebody from getting an EBT or SNAP benefit, because who would want to take food away from the hungry?"

Talcove proposed "a concerted effort" to remove suspicious stores from the SNAP program, upgrading EBT cards to chip technology, strengthening identity verification, and monitoring large purchases to detect potential fraud.

In a statement to Fox News Digital, a New York City Department of Social Services spokesperson said EBT skimming is a "nationwide crisis," saying the city has been working closely with state officials and law enforcement to help "vulnerable" New Yorkers who may be impacted.

Just this month, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul announced the state will begin replacing current EBT cards with more secure, chip-enabled cards in early 2027 to help stop fraud.

Following five years of investigations, Talcove praised the Secret Service but argued arrests alone won't solve the problem.

"It is just so part of the ecosystem now," he said.

Talcove said "once you see it, you can't unsee" the "unconscionable" fraud where businesses disguise themselves as normal stores while operating as something else entirely.

"How is it that a laundromat is pretending to be a place where you can buy food?" he said.

item.title: GOP erupts over 6,600 noncitizen voters found in blue state as scandal deepens: ‘Worse than anyone expected’
item.description: Republicans demand a halt to automatic voter registration through New Jersey's MVC after a software error registered 6,600 non-citizens to vote.
item.content:encoded:
Republican outrage went into overdrive after New Jersey Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill announced that the state had mistakenly registered 6,600 noncitizen voters, prompting calls for an immediate end to automatic voter registration through the New Jersey Motor Vehicle System (MVC).

Sherrill announced on Tuesday that a "serious software error in New Jersey’s Motor Vehicle System led to the registration of roughly 6,600 people who indicated that they were not U.S. citizens between June 2023 and June 2024, almost three years prior to my taking office." Sherrill said preliminary analysis indicates that 400 individuals voted due to the error.

Republican state Sen. Declan O'Scanlon told Fox News Digital that New Jersey Republicans have been raising concerns about the MVC registration system for years and "each time we were reassured that this ‘could never happen.’"

"It happened, and it’s worse than anyone expected," he said.

DEMOCRAT NEW JERSEY GOVERNOR SAYS THOUSANDS OF NONCITIZENS DISCOVERED ON VOTER ROLLS, PURGED

In light of the error, O’Scanlon called for a pause on all voter registrations through the MVC until a thorough investigation is completed for every registration processed through the system. He emphasized that beyond any software glitch, when state Democrats "made it law for noncitizens to obtain driver’s licenses, Republicans warned that this very problem could occur."

"If we know that more than 6,000 noncitizens were improperly registered to vote over two years, why on earth would we stop the investigation there? This system has been in place for years. We should be reviewing every registration processed through the MVC over the last decade," he told Fox News Digital. "Until that thorough investigation is complete, the MVC should suspend all voter registrations."

In a Tuesday news conference, Sherrill emphasized that the mistake did not occur while she was in office. She criticized her predecessor, former Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy, the MVC and the state’s software contractor, IDEMIA, for their alleged role in the scandal.

"It's unacceptable that the vendor released software with such a glaring error. It's unacceptable that the MVC took a year to get this issue fixed, and it's unacceptable that no one in the previous administration brought this to light," said Sherrill.

IDEMIA, however, released a statement pushing back on Sherrill’s assertion that its software is to blame.

The company said that its role is to "transmit information through the motor vehicle system" and that the New Jersey Department of State’s Division of Elections "is ultimately responsible for verifying eligibility to vote."

This comes as President Donald Trump and Republicans push for passage of the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) America Act, which proponents say would crack down on noncitizen voting.

NEW JERSEY DEM GOV'S BLAME GAME BACKFIRES AFTER TECH COMPANY PINS NONCITIZEN VOTER REGISTRATION ON STATE

Vice President JD Vance called on New Jersey senators to vote in favor of the SAVE America Act, writing in a tongue-in-cheek post on X, "If only there was a way to stop this type of ‘software error’ that allowed non-citizens to vote in our elections."

"I hope Senators @CoryBooker and @SenatorAndyKim will join us in voting to pass the SAVE America Act to ensure that only eligible American voters can participate in New Jersey elections, and all elections across the United States," wrote Vance.

New Jersey’s junior U.S. Sen. Andy Kim, however, said during an interview with MSNBC that "this is something that needs fixing, but it’s not this widespread systemic problem that Trump has been using and weaponizing to try to push forward a very extreme agenda," according to Breitbart.

Kim said that "this is unacceptable and this needs to be fixed immediately and needs people need to be held accountable," but added, "We also need to make sure that we’re looking at the problems in the right size, that this is not akin to what Donald Trump was talking about."

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

Ally Triolo, a spokesperson for the Republican National Committee, reacted by telling Fox News Digital that "Democrats’ refusal to acknowledge that the thousands of noncitizens found on New Jersey’s voter rolls represent exactly the kind of election integrity failure President Trump highlighted last week proves one thing: their hatred of the President matters more than doing what is right for voters and securing American elections once and for all."

Back in the state capitol in Trenton, New Jersey Assembly Republican Leader John DiMaio called for both a halt to automatic MVC registrations and a "top-to-bottom" audit of the state’s voter rolls. He believes the problem "is much more pervasive than 6,600 noncitizen voters."

"New Jersey Republicans have been sounding the alarm for years that the automatic voter registration process at the MVC was highly problematic," said DiMaio. "Until citizens are 100% assured that not one illegal resident will be mistakenly or intentionally registered to vote, the automatic process should be immediately halted."

He also called on Sherrill to "put politics aside for once and focus on election integrity" by cooperating with the federal government to "get all the non-citizens off the rolls."

CNN'S JAKE TAPPER HITS DEMS FOR REJECTING ILLEGAL VOTING CONCERNS AMID NEW JERSEY VOTER ROLL CONTROVERSY

In the state senate, Minority Leader Anthony Bucco told Fox News Digital that many of the issues stem from "the fact that New Jersey has been allowing undocumented immigrants to obtain a driver’s license since 2020."

"Over six years ago, Trenton Democrats chose to allow undocumented immigrants to obtain driver's licenses despite our opposition, and they placed voter registration in the hands of the MVC—an agency with a long history of administrative failures," he explained.

New Jersey Sen. Kristin Corrado, who serves as ranking member of the state Judiciary Committee, said that before the scandal broke out, she had sent six letters to the Murphy administration warning of voter registration issues.

"Democrats wrote this law, Democrats implemented this process, and Democrats failed to fix the obvious problems that followed," she said. "Now we are learning that a catastrophic government failure allowed thousands of non-citizens to be registered to vote, and suddenly everyone is acting surprised."

Republican Assemblyman Greg McGuckin likened the 400 illegal votes to "negating the votes of U.S. citizens."

"The problem is much larger than Democrats are willing to admit," he said.

Fox News Digital reached out to Sherrill, Kim, Booker, New Jersey Senate President Nicholas Scutari and General Assembly Speaker Craig Coughlin for comment.

item.title: Five impact freshmen from the SEC who are sure to turn heads on the field this coming season
item.description: From Brandon Arrington's blazing speed at Texas A&M to Jared Curtis flipping from Georgia to Vanderbilt, five SEC freshmen are poised to dominate.
item.content:encoded:
As we enter day three of SEC Media Days, the discussion continues to center around issues that are only tangentially related to what will happen on the field in 2026.

Everyone seems to be so preoccupied with whether the SEC will secede from college football that they forget we have an actual season to play in less than a month and a half.

Fear not, SEC fans. I'm here to talk some actual football with you all, and on today's dockett, I'll be keying in on five freshmen who will have the biggest impact on the field this fall.

GREG SANKEY ADDRESSES SEC BREAKAWAY TALK AS MICHIGAN TRIES TO STEAL SPOTLIGHT WITH AD WARDE MANUEL DECISION

Year in and year out, SEC teams recruit some of the best high school football prospects in the world, and 2026 was no different.

Plenty of blue-chip freshmen will see the field this coming year, but here are five first-year players you really need to keep an eye on.

I realize it may seem like I'm not going out on a limb picking the highest rated cornerback in the class of 2026 to make an impact early, and you're right.

But this isn't supposed to be a vehicle for hot takes, and former five-star Brandon Arrington is the real deal, so it's only natural that he finds himself on this list.

He might be the freakiest athlete on Texas A&M's roster, which is saying a lot considering the stockpile of talent they possess, but Arrington has every tool a DB could ever want.

He's 6-foot-2 with long arms and runs a blistering 10.21 in the 100-meter dash. That's STUPID fast.

THREE INTRIGUING QUARTERBACK BATTLES TO WATCH DURING COLLEGE FOOTBALL OFFSEASON CAMPS

The Aggies may have plenty of veteran leadership on defense, particularly at cornerback with the likes of Dezz Ricks, but Arrington is still good enough to break into the rotation as a true freshman.

If you like watching super athletic defensive backs (and I don't know why you wouldn't), then keep an eye on this young man.

Not many people outside of hardcore recruiting fans knew who Jayden Woods was prior to the 2025 season.

All he did was lead the defensive line in snaps played, tie for the team lead in sacks, and earn Freshman All-SEC honors for his efforts.

I bring up Woods because KJ Ford profiles as a similar type of player from a recruiting perspective, and their trajectories after arriving on campus have paralleled each other quite nicely.

Ford is slightly bigger at 6-foot-4 and 250 pounds and has the ideal body type for an SEC edge rusher.

Reports out of spring camp and summer workouts have indicated that Ford has been a monster both on the field and in the weight room, which is exactly what many were saying about Woods this time last year.

If there was ever a player from Florida's 2026 recruiting class that had Freshman All-SEC written all over him from day one, it's KJ Ford.

The Georgia Bulldogs have become somewhat of a tight end factory since the turn of the decade.

With players like Brock Bowers, Darnell Washington, and now Oscar Delp littering NFL rosters, UGA is slowly becoming TEU, and that trend looks like it will continue with their latest titan of a tight end.

Kaiden Prothro was one of the most sought-after tight ends in the country last recruiting cycle, choosing the Bulldogs over the likes of Alabama, Florida, and Texas.

It's easy to see why, too, as Prothro possesses a monstrous 6-foot-6 frame to go along with great long speed and massive 10-inch hands.

ED ORGERON-LANE KIFFIN PAIRING ALREADY PAYING DIVIDENDS FOR LSU TIGERS DURING PIVOTAL RECRUITING WEEKEND

Translation: he's a jump ball touchdown waiting to happen.

While he lacks the requisite beef to be an inline blocker in the SEC as a freshman, his athleticism and length will make him nearly impossible to keep off the field.

Georgia offensive coordinator Mike Bobo has to be salivating at the idea of using Prothro as a chess piece in his system, and the rest of the SEC likely just developed a migraine thinking about this young man in red and black for the next three or four years.

I heard so many people say Alabama just can't recruit like they used to, but after looking through their 2026 class and seeing mutants like Cederian Morgan, I can't see why anyone would say something like that.

Morgan could have gone anywhere in the country but chose to stay in state and play for the Tide, and Kalen DeBoer has to be thanking his lucky stars every night for that.

If you thought Ryan Williams was a beast as a freshman, Morgan might be even more college-ready at this point in his career from a physical standpoint.

He has the size you look for in a dominant number one receiver (6-foot-4 and 220 pounds), and has verified track speed and a silver medal in the Alabama 6A high jump to go along with it.

Bama will be looking for some firepower to help out one of their inexperienced quarterbacks this fall, and I can't think of a better option than a 6'4" Olympic-level athlete to throw the ball to in a tight spot.

Expect Morgan to see plenty of action early and often this fall, and I believe he will make the most of those opportunities.

If you've been paying attention to me since the start of SEC Media Days, you knew this was coming.

Jared Curtis was all set to go to Georgia before a change of heart found him staying put in Nashville to play for the hometown Vanderbilt Commodores.

Players of Curtis' pedigree don't pick the Vandy baseball cap off the table at the signing ceremony very often, but this could maybe signal a changing of the guard.

Regardless, Curtis has prototypical size for an SEC-ready quarterback at 6-foot-3 and 230 pounds and possesses a big arm as well as the creativity to make plays happen when the pocket breaks down.

He's an above average athlete as well, which he may need if things don't go according to plan early in his career at Vanderbilt.

Offensive coordinator Tim Beck was able to mold Diego Pavia into a Heisman finalist, so I will be very intrigued to see what he can do with a five-star ball of clay like Curtis.

There will be growing pains, but by the end of the season, I truly believe Jared Curtis will be one of the hottest freshman in the country.

item.title: Angels third baseman brings basketball onto the diamond, turns slick double play with unconventional move
item.description: Angels third baseman Vaughn Grissom's creative bounce pass helped Oswald Peraza avoid the sun and complete a smooth double play in the Angels' 5-1 win.
item.content:encoded:
Los Angeles Angels third baseman Vaughn Grissom brought some basketball onto the diamond.

In the top of the third inning of the Angels’ 5-1 win over the St. Louis Cardinals on Tuesday at Angel Stadium, Grissom brought the bounce pass into play.

With a runner on first base and one out, Cardinals catcher Iván Herrera hit a ground ball to Grissom at third base. The 25-year-old fielded it cleanly and went to throw to second base to begin the double play.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

However, the sun was directly in second baseman Oswald Peraza’s eyes. So Grissom, in a very unconventional move, fired the ball into the dirt halfway between him and second base so Peraza could catch the ball on the bounce and avoid the sun.

Peraza fielded Grissom’s bounce pass easily, turned and fired a strike to first baseman Nolan Schanuel to complete the slick double play.

ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!

The double play was a highlight in a game filled with strong pitching and defense during the team’s win. Angels starting pitcher Walbert Ureña threw six innings of one-run ball, striking out five hitters while giving up six hits. Ureña lowered his ERA to 2.78 with the performance.

The Angels jumped on the Cardinals early, as five of their 10 hits came in the first inning. Designated hitter Jorge Soler got the scoring started with a two-run double, Grissom drove in Soler with a line drive to right field, and right fielder Jo Adell punctuated the five-run first inning with a two-run home run.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

For the Angels, it was their fourth straight win, which is tied for their longest winning streak of the season. The Cardinals are trending in the other direction, as they have lost 10 of their last 14 games.

The Angels (41-61) will look to complete a three-game sweep of the Cardinals (51-49) when they play the final game of the set on Wednesday at 4:07 p.m. ET.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: Republicans sound alarm over far-left group's push to transform America into 'Cuban dictatorship'
item.description: The Democratic Socialists of America want to abolish the Senate and electoral college. Republican senators are firing back at the reform push.
item.content:encoded:
Senate Republicans are warning that a new policy push from a far-left group gaining momentum in politics would unravel the Constitution, and could leave them without a job.

As a progressive surge threatens to rewrite the core of the Democratic Party, earlier this month the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA), which scored early victories in primary elections across the U.S. over the last several weeks, unveiled its vision for government.

Among the three main pillars was a call to abolish the Senate in favor of an expanded House of Representatives with multiple parties in a bid to effectively eliminate the age-old, two-party system that has dominated American politics and representation for more than two centuries.

WARREN DEFIES SCHUMER AS DEM CIVIL WAR EXPLODES OVER MUST-WIN SENATE RACE

"I mean, we are important," Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., quipped to Fox News Digital. "[It’s] pretty disgusting. I mean, you know the Senate is here so we can represent all — make sure all the states are represented. So, yeah. I think the Founding Fathers were pretty smart."

While the number of members in the House of Representatives depends on the population of every state, the Senate is the great equalizer where every state, regardless of size or population, receives two senators to represent them in the upper chamber. 

The progressive swell during this midterm cycle, so far, has largely been contained to the House, where DSA-backed candidates toppled longtime Democratic incumbents in New York City and Colorado last month.

But one candidate, Abdul El-Sayed, who is running for the Democratic nomination in the Michigan Senate race, has tenuous links to the DSA from events the organization has sponsored that he’s attended. They've also not formally endorsed him in his battle against Rep. Haley Stevens, D-Mich.

WATCH: SURFACED VIDEOS OF DEM SENATE CANDIDATE BACKING 'DEFUND THE POLICE' CONTRADICT RECENT DENIALS

Still, he has the backing of two of the top DSA-aligned lawmakers in Congress: Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y.

The DSA’s program, dubbed Workers Deserve More, was officially launched last week on a call with DSA members and called to eliminate the upper chamber of Congress.

One of the group's top organizers, Michaela Branagan, contended, "Well, why do we have a Senate?"

"A lot of people are going to see that, and they’d be, like, ‘Wait a second. What’s so great about the Senate?’ I mean, I’m not sure about this, but I’m pretty sure the only popular senator is Bernie Sanders — because he ran for president as a Democratic Socialist," Branagan said on the call.

BERNIE SANDERS, DSA REVEAL DEMANDS FOR DEM PARTY AFTER SOCIALISTS SWEEP NEW YORK ELECTIONS

Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., told Fox News Digital that the DSA ought to test their vision of governance far from where anybody could see it.

"Maybe a little pilot project on an island nation where the entire world experiment is on somebody's shoulders, like, ‘ooh, I didn't get that one right,’" Tillis said. "My God. I'm pretty happy with being the exceptional nation on the planet right now. I'm into course correction, a little tuning there, trimming the sails, but not sinking the boat."

And Sen. Bernie Moreno, R-Ohio, questioned "where did this group of geniuses come from in America?"

He argued that the midterm election, and the schism within the Democratic Party, was turning the battle into not being about the GOP versus Democrats, but rather "people who hate this country versus people who love this country."

Moreno, who was born in Columbia before moving to Florida with his family as a child, said that he learned there was a "very specific reason the Senate was created" after his mother made him read the Federalist Papers before obtaining his citizenship.

"I think if somebody goes through that exercise they're going to say, ‘What were these guys thinking? Like, why was the Senate created? Why is it the way it is?’ They’ll understand it," Moreno said. "They also, remember, want to abolish the electoral college."

"Basically, they just want to have a Cuban dictatorship in America," he continued. "It hasn't worked out so well for Cuba."

item.title: Rep Cory Mills under active criminal investigation by the Department of Justice, source says
item.description: Rep. Cory Mills faces an active DOJ criminal investigation while the House Ethics Committee continues its probe into sexual misconduct and dating violence.
item.content:encoded:
Embattled Republican Rep. Cory Mills, R-Fla., is under active criminal investigation by the Department of Justice, Fox News has learned.

A source familiar with the matter told Fox News that the DOJ is investigating the Florida lawmaker, though the specifics of what he is being investigated for are unknown.

Mills, who serves on the House Armed Services and Intelligence committees, has separately been under investigation by the House Ethics Committee regarding allegations of campaign finance violations, sexual misconduct, and dating violence.

RESIGNATION IS THE NEW ESCAPE HATCH AS LAWMAKERS FACE EXPULSION

Mills faces allegations of domestic abuse from an incident in February 2025 and a separate case of blackmail from July that same year, where he allegedly threatened to release explicit images of a romantic partner.

Mills has denied any wrongdoing and hasn't been charged with any crimes.

The House Ethics Committee released a statement in April stating that its investigation into Mills that began in November 2025 was still ongoing. The panel noted at the time that its inquiry involves "sexual misconduct and/or dating violence."

REPUBLICAN LAWMAKER WELCOMES HOUSE ETHICS PROBE INTO SEXUAL MISCONDUCT ALLEGATIONS AGAINST HIM

The traditionally secretive panel did not specify a timeline for when it plans to wrap up the probe.

Rep. Nancy Mace, R-S.C., also filed a motion to expel Mills in April. The resolution accused Mills of misrepresenting his military service, sexual misconduct, campaign finance violations and illicit involvement in federal contracts as a member of Congress, among other charges.

Mills was first elected in 2022 and was re-elected in 2024. He is seeking a third House term this November.

Fox News Digital’s Adam Pack and Leo Briceno contributed to this report.

item.title: Woman risks being gored by bison for a selfie at Badlands National Park in shocking footage
item.description: Video shows a woman walking dangerously close to a massive bison in Badlands National Park to snap a selfie, risking being gored by the wild animal.
item.content:encoded:
A tourist is going viral for thinking it was a good idea to stand next to a massive bison.

As our loyal readers know, there are many people walking this planet who are seemingly desperate to win a Darwin Award.

One of the best ways to do it is to mess with wild animals out in nature, especially those with the size to cause serious injury or death.

TERRIFIED HIKERS FACE NIGHTMARE SURVIVAL SCENARIO AFTER HEART-POUNDING GRIZZLY BEAR ENCOUNTER

Enter another tourist lacking basic awareness.

The popular Instagram page @touronsofnationalparks recently posted a video that is so mind-boggling that it has to be seen to be believed.

A woman in Badlands National Park decided to walk up to a huge bison to get a selfie.

WOMAN ATTACKED BY BROWN BEAR WHILE HIKING WITH FRIEND AND 3 DOGS NEAR ALASKA STATE PARK

Imagine risking being gored for a selfie. Absolutely insane.

You can watch the shocking footage below, and let me know your thoughts at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

The comments on the video didn't disappoint at all, which is exactly what you'd expect from the internet.

WOMAN STALKED BY CHARGING BEAR ON MORNING DOG WALK CAPTURES TERRIFYING ENCOUNTER ON CAMERA

"How do [sic] die in 60 seconds part 1. 👏👏," one person wrote on the video.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

Another added, "People just have no idea! Think of it as a human stranger moving in on your personal space for a selfie and you don’t know them at all!"

A third added, "Play stupid games, win stupid prizes. 🙄"
